attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split
86	
87	JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.
88	
89	**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.
90	
91	**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).
92	
93	**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.
94	
95	**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.
96	
97	## References
98	
99	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
100	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
101	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
102	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
103

106	## Character
107	
108	Spec-uniformity sweep across the six cult-verb procedural sub-specs landed in BBAAZ. Pure .adoc — no code, no behavior change. File-disjoint targets → parallel agents. Suitable for landing while long test runs are in flight.
109	
110	## Drifts (from BBAAZ post-review notch 118c2d43)
111	
112	1. **Invest step-3 shape drift** — RBSDK and RBSRK use different headings + action-verb idioms for the SA-existence preflight. Pick one shape (RBSDK's `{rbbc_require}` HTTP-404 form is a reasonable target), apply to both.
113	
114	2. **Divest //axhoc_completion drift** — RBSDD and RBSRD completion blocks are non-parallel (different lengths; RBSRD has a "Proceed to invest" hint that RBSDD lacks). Pick one parallel template, apply to both.
115	
116	3. **Divest step-4 RBRA-removal phrasing drift** — RBSDD describes the cloud-state-mirroring rationale in a NOTE block; RBSRD has it inline. Align placement.
117	
118	4. **NOTE-block typographic style** — the six BBAAZ-authored specs use inline NOTE prose with linked terms interpolated mid-line; gold-standard RBSDE/RBSDV use line-broken NOTE prose with linked terms on their own lines. Convert the six new specs to line-broken form for typographic consistency with the rest of the procedural-spec corpus.

121	
122	- BBAAZ post-review notch 118c2d43 — surfaced these drifts during review of the parallel-agent work.
123	- RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc, RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc — line-broken NOTE-block reference shape.

127	Code, behavioral changes, contract semantics, other procedural specs (RBSDE/RBSDU/RBSGM/RBSDV/RBSDY).

## Mounted ₣BB ₢BBAAb

**Heat:** rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (15/23, racing) — broader work is the `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine third tier with marshal-zero entry contract. This pace sits to the side of that arc as a docs-only cleanup, parallel-safe with coding paces.

**Pace:** spec-cult-verb-procedural-uniformity (₢BBAAb) [rough] — pure .adoc sweep across 6 cult-verb procedural specs (RBSDK invest, RBSRK invest, RBSDD divest, RBSRD divest, RBSDR roster, RBSRL roster). Four drifts surfaced by BBAAZ post-review notch:

1. **Invest step-3 shape** — RBSDK vs RBSRK preflight differ in heading + idiom; pick one (RBSDK `{rbbc_require}` HTTP-404 is suggested target)
2. **Divest //axhoc_completion** — RBSDD vs RBSRD completion blocks non-parallel; pick template
3. **Divest step-4 RBRA-removal phrasing** — NOTE-block placement drift between RBSDD and RBSRD
4. **NOTE-block style** — convert all 6 to line-broken form matching gold-standard RBSDE / RBSDV

**Out of scope:** code, behavior, contract semantics, RBSDE/RBSDU/RBSGM/RBSDV/RBSDY.

### Proposed approach

- **Step 1 (main thread, sonnet-ish read work):** read RBSDE + RBSDV to lock the line-broken NOTE-block reference shape; read RBSDK/RBSRK side-by-side to nail the invest-preflight target shape; read RBSDD/RBSRD side-by-side for divest completion + RBRA-removal target shapes. Decisions belong here, not in the agents.
- **Step 2 (parallel sonnet agents, file-disjoint):** dispatch one agent per file (6 agents) with explicit "here's the target pattern, here's what to change" briefings. Mechanical application only — no judgment delegated.
- **Step 3 (main thread):** spot-check each file, grep-verify no broken xrefs, notch with explicit file list.

**Strategy summary:** Main-thread design (opus), parallel sonnet for mechanical apply. File-disjoint with concurrent coding officium → safe to fan out. No code touch, so no build/test pass needed beyond a final asciidoc sanity grep.

Name check: "spec-cult-verb-procedural-uniformity" fits the docket precisely. No rename needed.

133	//axhos_step

135	{rbtr_mason}

138	{rbtr_mason}

142	... accountId: `mason-«RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
143	... displayName: `Mason for RB Depot: «RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
144	... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
145	.. {rbbc_store}
146	`«MASON_EMAIL»` from response `.email` field

148	//axhos_step

150	{rbtr_mason}
151	Permissions*:
152	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo} on `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`

154	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
155	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/storage.objectViewer`
156	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} on `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
157	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/viewer`
158	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/logging.logWriter`

162	{rbtr_mason}*:

165	.. Grant role: `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`
166	.. NOTE: Cloud Build service agent is Google-provisioned (auto-created when Cloud Build API is enabled), not a user-created service account. The `tokenCreator` role enables build steps running as {rbtr_mason} (via the build JSON's `serviceAccount` field) to generate fresh access tokens for GCP API calls — required for GAR authentication (docker login, crane push). This is distinct from `serviceAccountUser` (actAs), which the {rbtr_director} holds on {rbtr_mason} for submitting builds.
167	.. {rbbc_require}
168	IAM binding successful

171	{rbtgi_depot}
172	. *Confirm Depot Creation*:
173	.. {rbbc_show}

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_vouch}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation vouches for every *pending*
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository},
8	where *pending* means a hallmark whose subtree contains both
9	{rbtga_ark_image}
10	and
11	{rbtga_ark_about}
12	basenames but no
13	{rbtga_ark_vouch}.
14	The
15	{rbtr_director}
16	enumerates the full hallmark corpus via a single GAR REST `packages.list` call (the same shared helper used by
17	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}),
18	identifies pending hallmarks, resolves the vessel name from each hallmark's
19	{rbtga_ark_about}
20	metadata (since vessel identity no longer lives in the GAR path), and dispatches
21	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
22	per hallmark.
23	Mode-awareness (conjure, bind, graft) lives inside
24	{rbtgo_ark_vouch};
25	batch vouch does not branch on mode.
26	
27	NOTE: *Fail-fast semantics.* If
28	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
29	fails for any hallmark, the batch terminates via fatal error at that hallmark.
30	There is no skip-with-warning path and no summary-count-masking of failures: a broken hallmark demands immediate attention, not quiet continuation.
31	
32	The
33	{rbtr_director}
34	batch-vouches arks:
35	
36	//axhos_step
37	. *Authenticate as
38	{rbtr_director}*:
39	.. Execute
40	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
41	.. {rbbc_store}
42	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
43	
44	//axhos_step
45	. *Enumerate Hallmark Packages*:
46	.. {rbbc_call}
47	the shared hallmark enumeration helper — a single
48	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list]
49	call under `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/` (`RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT`), filtered to `<hallmark>/<basename>` shape
50	.. {rbbc_store}
51	`«PAIRS»` as sorted `<hallmark> <basename>` pairs (same shape produced for
52	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally})
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Identify Pending Hallmarks (Pass 1)*:
56	.. Iterate `«PAIRS»` in sort order; each contiguous run of the same hallmark is one
57	{rbtga_hallmark}'s
58	ark inventory.
59	.. For each hallmark, record whether
60	{rbtga_ark_image},
61	{rbtga_ark_about},
62	and
63	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
64	each appear in its run.
65	.. A hallmark is *pending* when `image` and `about` are present and `vouch` is absent.
66	Collect pending hallmarks into `«PENDING»` (in-memory sequence).
67	.. If `«PENDING»` is empty, the procedure completes successfully with no vouches submitted.
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Resolve Vessel and Vouch Per Pending Hallmark (Pass 2)*:
71	.. For each `«HALLMARK»` in `«PENDING»`:
72	... Extract the hallmark's
73	{rbtga_ark_about}
74	artifact (package `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«HALLMARK»/about`, tag `«HALLMARK»`) to a scratch directory via the shared GAR artifact extraction helper
75	... Read `.vessel_name` from the extracted `build_info.json` via `jq`
76	... {rbbc_store}
77	`«VESSEL_DIR»` as `«RBRR_VESSEL_DIR»/«VESSEL_NAME»`
78	... {rbbc_require}
79	`«VESSEL_DIR»` exists on disk — the vessel named in `about` metadata must correspond to a configured vessel in the current regime
80	... {rbbc_fatal}
81	if the `about` artifact cannot be extracted, `build_info.json` is missing, `.vessel_name` is empty, or `«VESSEL_DIR»` does not exist — the `about` artifact is the sole source of vessel identity for this batch; a gap there is a fail-fast stop
82	... Invoke
83	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
84	with `«VESSEL_DIR»` and `«HALLMARK»`.
85	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
86	loads the vessel's
87	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
88	internally and dispatches the appropriate verification (conjure: SLSA DSSE verification; bind: digest-pin; graft: GRAFTED stamp).
89	... {rbbc_fatal}
90	if
91	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
92	fails — the batch terminates at the broken hallmark.
93	
94	//axhoot_typed_output
95	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
96	. *Display Summary*:
97	.. {rbbc_show}
98	`Batch vouch complete — N/N hallmark(s) vouched` where N is the initial pending count
99	
100	//axhoc_completion
101	Successful execution creates one
102	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
103	artifact per pending hallmark (N total, where N = initial pending count).
104	The batch runs sequentially; a failure at any hallmark halts the remainder.
105	+
106	*Scope of vessel resolution*: Reading `vessel_name` from `build_info.json` is a narrow anticipation of the broader vessel-from-metadata pattern (AAL territory) — applied locally inside batch vouch only, because
107	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
108	's signature still takes a vessel directory.
109	Other operations (tally, plumb, abjure) continue to not surface vessel.
110	+
111	*Two-pass shape*: Pass 1 collects pending hallmarks into memory before Pass 2 begins processing.
112	Each
113	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
114	invocation sources credentials and issues Cloud Build calls that would silently consume loop stdin if the pending set were streamed; the in-memory collection decouples enumeration from dispatch.
115	+
116	*Idempotency*: Re-running batch vouch after a successful batch finds an empty pending set (every prior pending hallmark now has `vouch` present) and exits with no action.
117	+
118	*No mode pre-check*: Earlier shapes of this operation pre-probed SLSA provenance for conjure-mode hallmarks to skip-with-warning those lacking provenance.
119	That pattern is gone — all mode-specific verification happens inside
120	{rbtgo_ark_vouch},
121	and any failure there surfaces as a fail-fast stop rather than being absorbed into a summary count.
122

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_invest}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation invests a {rbtr_director} {giam_service_account} into the {rbtgi_depot_project}.
5	The {rbtr_governor} mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
6	The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
7	Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must {rbtgo_director_divest} first to re-key.
8	
9	//axhos_step
10	. *Authenticate as
11	{rbtr_governor}*:
12	.. Execute
13	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
14	.. {rbbc_store}
15	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
16	
17	//axhos_step
18	. *Derive
19	{rbtr_director}
20	Service Account Identity*:
21	.. {rbbc_store}
22	`«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»` as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (singleton per role per depot, regime-kindled via
23	{rbrr_depot_moniker})
24	.. {rbbc_store}
25	`«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` as `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»@${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com` (depot project ID derived from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`)
26	
27	//axhos_step
28	. *Preflight: Confirm Service Account Does Not Exist*:
29	.. {rbbc_call}
30	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
31	.. {rbbc_fatal}
32	if HTTP 200: service account already exists — operator must execute
33	{rbtgo_director_divest}
34	before re-investing this role
35	.. {rbbc_require}
36	HTTP 404 to proceed
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Create
40	{rbtr_director}
41	Service Account*:
42	.. {rbbc_call}
43	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
44	... accountId: `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»`
45	... displayName: `Recipe Bottle Director`
46	... description: `Create/destroy container images for depot ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`
47	... project:
48	{rbtgi_depot_project}
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	HTTP 200 response
51	.. {rbbc_store}
52	`«DIRECTOR_UNIQUE_ID»` from response `.uniqueId` field
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Verify Service Account Propagation*:
56	.. {rbbc_call}
57	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
58	.. {rbbc_poll}
59	with backoff until HTTP 200 (newly-created SA may not be immediately resolvable by email)
60	.. NOTE: Poll must use the email address, not the numeric ID. The email-based lookup path has independent propagation from the UID-based path, and subsequent steps (keys.list, keys.create) all use the email address. Polling by UID can succeed while the email path still returns 404.
61	.. {rbbc_require}
62	service account accessible by email
63	
64	//axhos_step
65	. *Preflight: Ensure Pristine Key State*:
66	.. {rbbc_call}
67	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/list[serviceAccounts.keys.list] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
68	.. {rbbc_store}
69	`«EXISTING_KEYS»` from response `.keys[]`
70	.. {rbbc_require}
71	no keys with `keyType: USER_MANAGED` exist
72	.. {rbbc_fatal}
73	if USER_MANAGED keys found: "Manual cleanup required via Console"
74	
75	//axhos_step
76	. *Generate Service Account Key*:
77	.. {rbbc_call}
78	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/create[serviceAccounts.keys.create] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with:
79	... privateKeyType: `TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE`
80	.. Retry up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX` times on HTTP 404 with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts
81	.. NOTE: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) may succeed while keys.create POST returns 404 "does not exist" due to write-path propagation delay. Google link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[documents]: "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account." The 404 confirms no key was created, making retry safe. See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
82	.. {rbbc_store}
83	`«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` from response `.privateKeyData` field
84	.. {rbtoe_rbra_generate}
85	converts `«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` to `«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»`
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Get Depot Project Number*:
89	.. {rbbc_call}
90	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for
91	{rbtgi_depot_project}
92	.. {rbbc_store}
93	`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.projectNumber` field
94	
95	//axhos_step
96	. *Grant Project-Level Permissions*:
97	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} for
98	{rbtgi_depot_project}
99	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
100	... `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor` (submit and manage builds)
101	... `roles/viewer` (read project resources)
102	... `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` (use private pool for vouch `builds.create`)
103	.. Policy version: 3
104	.. {rbbc_require}
105	IAM policy update successful
106	
107	//axhos_step
108	. *Grant
109	{rbtr_mason}
110	Impersonation*:
111	.. NOTE:
112	{rbtr_director}
113	submits builds that execute as
114	{rbtr_mason}.
115	This requires `serviceAccountUser` permission on
116	{rbtr_mason}.
117	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on
118	{rbtr_mason}
119	service account
120	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`
121	.. {rbbc_require}
122	IAM binding successful
123	
124	//axhos_step
125	. *Grant
126	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
127	Access*:
128	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on
129	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
130	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
131	... `roles/storage.objectCreator` (upload sources)
132	... `roles/storage.objectViewer` (verify uploads)
133	.. {rbbc_require}
134	bucket IAM update successful
135	
136	//axhos_step
137	. *Grant
138	{rbtgi_repository}
139	Admin Access*:
140	.. {rbbc_call}
141	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/getIamPolicy[repositories.getIamPolicy] via HTTP GET with `options.requestedPolicyVersion=3` query parameter
142	.. NOTE: {gar_registry} proto specifies GET for `getIamPolicy` with no `additional_bindings` for POST. Using POST fails intermittently with HTTP 404 (transcoding layer rejection, not missing repository). See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
143	.. {rbbc_require}
144	HTTP 200 — 404 means repository does not exist (real error, not empty policy)
145	.. {rbbc_call}
146	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/setIamPolicy[repositories.setIamPolicy] for
147	{rbtgi_repository}
148	.. *Complete policy write*: Add ALL expected bindings to the read policy, regardless of what the read returned:
149	... `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin` — Director manages images
150	... `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer` — Mason pushes images at build time
151	.. NOTE: Same complete-policy-write pattern as the secret IAM step. Prevents stale read from overwriting Mason's writer binding set by {rbtgo_depot_levy}.
152	.. {rbbc_require}
153	repository IAM set with all expected bindings
154	
155	//axhoot_typed_output
